description: A fast-growing, peppery salad leaf that is incredibly easy to grow in pots. Regular picking keeps the leaves young and tender. Ideally grown in cooler weather (spring/autumn) as heat makes it bolt and turn spicy.

characteristics.waterNeeds: Moderate — keep moist to prevent bolting

pinchOutTops.info: Not typically required for this plant.

care.sowIndoors: Sow thinly in trays or modules in March. Plant out when large enough to handle. Useful for an early crop.
care.sowOutdoors: Sow thinly directly into pots from April. Cover lightly with soil. Keep moist.
care.hardenOff: If started indoors, harden off for a few days before planting out.
care.plantOut: Plant out seedlings or sow direct. Rocket doesn't mind crowding.
care.watering: Critical! Keep soil consistently moist. Dry soil triggers bolting (flowering) and makes leaves tough and bitter.
care.feeding: Feed with a general liquid fertiliser every 3-4 weeks.
care.harvest: Pick leaves when young (10cm long). Pick often to encourage regrowth.
care.overwintering: Hardy annual. Late sowings can be harvested into winter if protected from severe cold.

propagation.seedDescription: Small, round, brownish seeds. Very fast to germinate.

deadheading.notes: If it flowers, the leaves become bitter. You can eat the flowers though! Remove flower spikes to extend leaf harvest slightly.

seedSaving.method: Let a few plants flower and produce long pods. When pods are dry and brown, harvest and shell the seeds.
seedSaving.seedPodDescription: Long, thin pods containing many small round seeds.

troubleshooting: Flea beetle — tiny holes in leaves. Cover with fleece to prevent.
Bolting — flowering prematurely due to heat or dryness. Keep watered and shaded in summer.
Bitter taste — usually due to heat or old leaves.
funFact: Rocket has been grown in the Mediterranean since Roman times and was once considered an aphrodisiac!
